workflow.desc: Identify business module entry directories for each platform

field.name: sync_state_bizs_dir
field.desc: Absolute path to entry-dirs JSON output directory

field.name: configs_dir
field.desc: Absolute path to configuration files directory

block.field: Use the provided absolute paths directly. DO NOT construct or derive paths yourself.
All entryDirs paths must use forward slashes / as path separators (even on Windows)
Do not include leading or trailing slashes in entryDirs paths

block.desc: techStack values MUST match tech-stack-mappings.json keys
block.field: The techStack array values MUST exactly match keys defined in tech-stack-mappings.json (e.g., "fastapi", "vue3", "uniapp"). DO NOT prefix with language name (e.g., use "fastapi" NOT "python-fastapi", use "express" NOT "node-express"). The tech_identifier input parameter value should be used as the primary techStack entry.
DO NOT ask user "Should I continue?" or "How would you like to proceed?" during execution
DO NOT offer options like "Full execution / Partial / Stop" 鈥?always execute ALL tasks to completion
DO NOT suggest "Due to context window limits, let me pause" 鈥?complete current task, use checkpoint for resumption
DO NOT estimate workload and suggest breaking it into phases 鈥?execute ALL items in sequence
DO NOT warn about "large number of files" or "this may take a while" 鈥?proceed with generation
Context window management: if approaching limit, save progress to checkpoint file and resume 鈥?do NOT ask user for guidance

sequence.name: Process Platforms
sequence.desc: Iterate each platform to identify entry directories

block.desc: Iterate each platform to identify entry directories
Read each platform's sourcePath directory structure (3 levels deep)
block.field: MUST use Get-ChildItem (NOT tree command). MUST use ${platform.sourcePath} absolute path (NOT relative path). Scan depth follows module_scan.depth configuration from tech-stack-mappings.json.

block.desc: Analyze directory tree and identify entry directories based on platform type

field.name: logic_module_scan
field.value: Read tech-stack-mappings.json for the techStack's module_scan configuration. Use module_scan.root as the scan starting point and module_scan.depth as the grouping level (depth=1 means first-level subdirectories = one module each)

field.name: logic_backend
field.value: Find all directories containing *Controller.java or *Controller.kt files under module_scan.root. These are API entry directories. Module name = the business package name of the entry directory. Apply module_scan.depth for grouping level

field.name: logic_frontend_vue_react
field.value: Find directories under module_scan.root (e.g., src/views or src/pages). First-level subdirectories under module_scan.root are business modules when depth=1

field.name: logic_mobile_uniapp
field.value: Find first-level subdirectories under module_scan.root (e.g., src/pages). Plus top-level pages-* directories (module name = directory name without pages- prefix)

field.name: logic_mobile_miniprogram
field.value: Find first-level subdirectories under module_scan.root (e.g., pages) as modules

block.desc: Read tech-stack-mappings.json to load exclusion patterns
Check if identified_entries is not empty
Apply exclusion rules to filter out technical directories

field.name: exclusions_pure_technical
field.value: config, framework, enums, exception, util, utils, common, constant, constants, type, types, dto, vo, entity, model, mapper, repository, dao, service, impl

field.name: root_handling
field.value: Assign entry files not under any subdirectory to _root module

block.desc: Generate entry-dirs JSON file for the platform

block.field: The generated entry-dirs JSON file MUST strictly follow this structure: - Root object MUST contain "modules" field (array type) - Root object MUST NOT contain "businessModules", "subModules", or "components" fields - Each module in "modules" array MUST have exactly two fields: "name" (string) and "entryDirs" (array of strings) - For platforms with hierarchical directory structure (e.g. frontend views/system/user/), flatten into individual modules - Module names should use hyphen-separated composite names for sub-modules (e.g. "system-user", "system-role") - Multiple modules MUST NOT share the same entryDirs value. If multiple business areas share the same directory, they belong to ONE module. - If the generated JSON does not match this format, you MUST regenerate it before proceeding
Output is strictly the entry-dirs JSON file at the specified output path. Per agentflow-spec.md "Strict Block Adherence" rule, no extra files are permitted.

- modules array is not empty - each module has at least one entry directory - module names are business-meaningful (not technical terms like config, util) - entryDirs paths are correct and accessible - JSON format is valid
Entry directory recognition failed for platform ${platform.platformId}

block.desc: Re-analyze the directory tree due to validation failure

field.name: generated_files
field.desc: List of all generated entry-dirs JSON files

field.name: validation_summary
field.desc: Summary of validation results for all platforms
